questions.question: What is the default value of flex-wrap?
questions.explanation: flex-wrap defaults to nowrap, meaning items stay on one line.

questions.question: Which flex-wrap value causes items to overflow the container?
questions.explanation: nowrap keeps items on one line, which can cause overflow if they don't fit.

questions.question: Which property becomes available only when flex-wrap causes multiple lines?
questions.explanation: align-content controls distribution of lines when there are multiple lines (wrapping).

questions.question: What does wrap-reverse do differently from wrap?
questions.explanation: wrap-reverse reverses the cross axis direction, so lines stack from bottom to top.

questions.question: Which shorthand combines flex-direction and flex-wrap?
questions.explanation: flex-flow is the shorthand for flex-direction and flex-wrap.

questions.question: With flex-wrap: wrap and flex-direction: row, how do new lines form?
questions.explanation: With wrap, new lines appear below (in the cross axis direction).

questions.question: What happens to items when the container is too narrow with flex-wrap: wrap?
questions.explanation: With flex-wrap: wrap, items will wrap to new lines when there isn't enough space.

questions.question: Is flex-flow: column wrap a valid CSS declaration?
options: No, wrap only works with row
questions.explanation: flex-flow: column wrap is valid; items wrap to new columns.

questions.question: What is a common responsive design pattern using flex-wrap?
options: Flex items with flex-basis that creates a minimum width
questions.explanation: flex-basis sets a minimum width for items, and when combined with wrap, creates responsive rows.

questions.question: How many lines will items form with flex-wrap: nowrap?
questions.explanation: nowrap keeps all items on a single line, regardless of container width.

questions.question: Which value of flex-wrap would you use for a responsive image gallery?
questions.explanation: wrap allows images to flow onto multiple rows as the viewport shrinks.

questions.question: What is the correct syntax for flex-flow shorthand equivalent to 'column wrap'?
questions.explanation: flex-flow takes direction first, then wrap: flex-flow: column wrap;

questions.question: Does flex-wrap affect alignment on the main axis?
options: No, it only affects the cross axis behavior
questions.explanation: flex-wrap controls cross axis wrapping behavior, not main axis alignment.

questions.question: With flex-wrap: wrap-reverse, where do items first appear?
questions.explanation: wrap-reverse places the first line at the bottom of the container.

questions.question: Can you use gap with flex-wrap?
options: No, gap doesn't work with wrap
Yes, gap works perfectly with wrapped items
questions.explanation: gap works with flex-wrap, creating consistent spacing between wrapped items.

questions.question: What does flex-flow: row nowrap do?
options: Items stay in a single row and don't wrap
questions.explanation: row nowrap is the default: items flow in a row and don't wrap.

questions.question: When using flex-wrap: wrap, what determines when items wrap?
options: Total width of items vs container width
questions.explanation: Items wrap when their total width (including gap) exceeds the container width.

questions.question: Which of these enables responsive behavior without media queries?
questions.explanation: flex-wrap: wrap with a flex-basis (200px) creates responsive rows that wrap automatically.

questions.question: What happens if you set flex-wrap: wrap on a container with flex-direction: column?
options: Items wrap horizontally to new columns
Items wrap vertically to new rows
questions.explanation: With column direction, wrap creates new columns (horizontal wrapping).

questions.question: Is flex-wrap: wrap-reverse recommended for content where reading order matters?
options: No, it can confuse readers as stacking order is reversed
questions.explanation: wrap-reverse reverses the visual stacking order, which can confuse reading order.
